current_state.title: The ASI Stack
current_state.subtitle: A Governed Systems Architecture for Advanced AI, with ASI as the Stress Case
current_state.positioning: governed advanced-AI systems architecture; ASI is the extreme stress case rather than an achieved state
current_state.former_title: The ASI Stack: A Systems Architecture for Governed, Efficient, Self-Improving AI
current_state.credibility_risk: ASI remains prominent in the brand, so all public framing must preserve the stress-case boundary and current argument-level support state.

provisional_rule: The reframed subtitle is canonical for the active v1.x development line. Historical v1.0.0 release records retain the former subtitle and must not be rewritten.
recommendation_for_consideration: Decision implemented: retain The ASI Stack and reframe the subtitle so governed advanced-AI systems are the subject and ASI is the stress case.

options.label: Retain current title
options.description: Keep The ASI Stack and the current subtitle, relying on the compact trust surface and non-claims to manage interpretation.
benefits: Maximum continuity with v1.0.0 citations, URLs, figures, issue history, and existing recognition.
risks: Highest credibility tax with skeptical technical audiences.
Readers may infer implemented ASI capability before reaching the evidence boundary.
options.reversal_cost: Low now; grows as citations and releases accumulate.

details.subtitle: A Systems Architecture for Governed, Efficient, Self-Improving AI

options.label: Retain brand, reframe
options.description: Keep The ASI Stack but use a subtitle that names governed advanced-AI systems and presents ASI as the stress case.
benefits: Preserves the distinctive project identity and v1.0 lineage.
Moves the operational subject from achieved ASI toward governed systems architecture.
Can be introduced as a major-version positioning change with citation aliases.
risks: ASI remains prominent and may still dominate first impressions.
Subtitle migration must be exact across source, citation, metadata, figures, releases, and external listings.
options.reversal_cost: Medium; requires one governed metadata migration and preserved former-title citation note.

details.candidate_subtitle: A Governed Systems Architecture for Advanced AI, with ASI as the Stress Case
details.asi_role: stress case rather than achieved state

options.label: Rename governed AI stack
options.description: Rename the work The Governed AI Stack and retain ASI as a motivating limit case inside the book.
benefits: Most precise match to implemented artifacts and current evidence.
Lower barrier for systems, governance, and safety readers who reject ASI framing.
Makes applicability to current agentic systems immediate.
risks: Loses distinctive ASI-first brand and creates citation/title discontinuity.
May understate the long-horizon self-improvement and capability stress case.
options.reversal_cost: High after publication; requires former-title aliases and a major-version record.

details.candidate_subtitle: A Systems Architecture for Advanced, Efficient, Self-Improving AI

options.label: Rename as systems book
options.description: Use a conventional systems title and treat The ASI Stack as a historical/project-series label.
benefits: Lowest hype exposure and strongest conventional technical positioning.
Leaves room for broader current-system examples and external adoption.
risks: Largest identity loss and migration burden.
Generic title may reduce discoverability and understate the unifying architectural thesis.
options.reversal_cost: Very high; should occur only at a clearly recorded major-version boundary.

details.candidate_subtitle: Contracts, Evidence, and Control for Advanced AI
details.asi_role: historical project label and stress case

implementation_gate: Selection is recorded as retain_brand_reframe_subtitle for v1.x-development.
book_structure.json is the active title source; Quarto, README, landing, prompt, outline, and current citation metadata must agree.
Historical v1.0.0 release records and reader artifacts preserve the former title as version history.
Clean render, canonical-status, link, title-consistency, build, deployment, and public attestation gates must pass before this migration is called deployed.
non_claims: The author selected positioning; this packet does not claim that a deployment or immutable release has already completed.
Positioning does not prove the architecture, ASI, novelty, safety, or readiness.
